articles.title: On Iran, Trump Keeps World Off Balance With Ever-Changing Threats
articles.summary: Global leaders are struggling in their efforts to find a way to end the American-Israeli war on Iran, and they are spooked about what President Trump might do next.

articles.title: The Latest Blows to Iran’s Leadership
articles.summary: An Iranian intelligence chief killed overnight on Monday was one of several Iranian officials who occupied their posts for only a few months.

articles.summary: For the families of the Artemis II astronauts, the mission “begins at assignment.”

articles.title: Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
articles.summary: Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.

articles.title: She paid into Medicare for years. Trump's immigration policy will end her coverage

articles.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
articles.summary: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

articles.title: Iran pushes back against Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran's top officials pushed back against President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

articles.title: Newly Obtained Video of Minneapolis Shooting Undermines ICE Account
articles.summary: Prosecutors did not watch video of the nonfatal shooting until weeks after charging the wounded man, an official said.

articles.title: Plunging International Student Enrollment Under Trump Squeezes Colleges
articles.summary: The Trump administration’s campaign to curtail international students is not just hitting the elite schools targeted by the government.

articles.title: These blind students say their college blocked their education. A new rule could help
articles.summary: Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.

articles.title: Shingles can hit younger than you think. The vaccine can prevent excruciating pain
articles.summary: A reactivation of the virus that causes chickenpox, the illness can be miserable. Here's what to know about early warning signs, long-term symptoms and some surprising news about the vaccine.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to the 'Today' show months after her mother's disappearance
articles.summary: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.

articles.title: A music festival booked Kanye West, now known as Ye, and lost major sponsors
articles.summary: The rapper Ye was announced as the headliner for the Wireless Festival in London. He's gained notoriety over the years for his antisemitic comments and activities glorifying Nazis.

articles.title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
articles.summary: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

articles.title: ICE Agents Detain Newlywed Spouse of Soldier Training to Deploy
articles.summary: The 22-year-old wife of an Army staff sergeant came to the U.S. as a toddler. She was taken from a military base where the couple planned to live.

articles.title: Will Hungary's far-right leader Viktor Orban be voted out of power?
articles.summary: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.

articles.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

articles.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted

articles.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
articles.summary: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

articles.title: Local culture shapes the Zairean liturgy
articles.summary: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual
